title: Musical Version Of NGE
wikitext:
[]: Musical Version Of NGE             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Fri Nov 10, 2006 5:16 pm Ok so that title has got your attention now before you all kill me and remove various organs from my body and replace them with every day house hold objects, I don't mean like Shinji and Co all singing songs at each other with dialogue and such. Actually i'm thinking of this with another Musical Version in mind, namely the War Of The Worlds Musical Version by Jeff Wayne. I'm just watching the Live Version now with the CGI Sequences in the background and the 30 foot fighting machine and wonder just what could be done if someone decided to give NGE this sort of treatment, I think it'd be very hard to get right because Eva is a more complex story then WOTWs but can you imagine the stage show they'd have for it. So what do we think? Remember I'm thinking more in the style of the WOTWs Musical and not your standard Theater Musical.

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Fri Nov 10, 2006 5:51 pm i got a better one: Neon Genesis Evangelion on Ice

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Nov 10, 2006 5:58 pm Preemptive:

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Fri Nov 10, 2006 6:00 pm A nice simple "No" would have sufficed, although I have to ask have you actually heard the Musical Version Of War Of The Worlds so that you actually know what I'm talking about?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Nov 10, 2006 6:09 pm jcmoorehead wrote: A nice simple "No" would have sufficed, although I have to ask have you actually heard the Musical Version Of War Of The Worlds so that you actually know what I'm talking about?

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Fri Nov 10, 2006 6:39 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: jcmoorehead wrote: A nice simple "No" would have sufficed, although I have to ask have you actually heard the Musical Version Of War Of The Worlds so that you actually know what I'm talking about?

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Fri Nov 10, 2006 7:49 pm someone beat you to it http://www.fansview.com/2005/may2005/az28c.html

[]:             Quote Post by Millions Livio » Fri Nov 10, 2006 8:19 pm jcmoorehead wrote: A nice simple "No" would have sufficed.

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Sat Nov 11, 2006 5:15 am Evangelionfreak wrote: someone beat you to it http://www.fansview.com/2005/may2005/az28c.html

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Sat Nov 11, 2006 5:34 am this all cute and stuff...but, of course, no ones giving Eva on Ice any traction... dont you know how much Disney Profited when they added the Incredibles to Disney on Ice...theres money in this ice stuff

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Sat Nov 11, 2006 5:54 am master0rolando wrote: this all cute and stuff...but, of course, no ones giving Eva on Ice any traction... dont you know how much Disney Profited when they added the Incredibles to Disney on Ice...theres money in this ice stuff

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sat Nov 11, 2006 7:42 am That would have to be one big ass rink.

[]:             Quote Post by LordBlank » Sat Nov 11, 2006 8:59 am Hunter21 wrote: That would have to be one big ass rink.

[]: Re: Musical Version Of NGE             Quote Post by Charizard » Fri Oct 28, 2016 2:35 pm Reviving this thread from the dead (Evageeks seems to be a haven for ancient threads) How about a musical that focuses more on story, character and special effects than musical numbers? Any of the songs would be in the form of internal monologues exploring the characters' struggles and psychological states, thus making the musical of the "All In Their Heads" variety. Hell, maybe they can even be sung by non-actor singers who come on stage while the character actors continue going through the events and action (though having the characters sing could work too). Something like these: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=p0j5XWnb8aE https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5I9XkHgveyg So don't worry, Gendo won't be singing catchy, toe-tapping showtunes about Human Instrumentality :P (A good or a bad thing, depending on your point of view)

[]: Re: Musical Version Of NGE             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Fri Oct 28, 2016 3:28 pm Charizard wrote: So don't worry, Gendo won't be singing catchy, toe-tapping showtunes about Human Instrumentality :P (A good or a bad thing, depending on your point of view)

[]: Re: Musical Version Of NGE             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Fri Oct 28, 2016 5:30 pm Charizard wrote: Reviving this thread from the dead (Evageeks seems to be a haven for ancient threads) How about a musical that focuses more on story, character and special effects than musical numbers? Any of the songs would be in the form of internal monologues exploring the characters' struggles and psychological states, thus making the musical of the "All In Their Heads" variety. Hell, maybe they can even be sung by non-actor singers who come on stage while the character actors continue going through the events and action (though having the characters sing could work too). Something like these: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=p0j5XWnb8aE https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5I9XkHgveyg So don't worry, Gendo won't be singing catchy, toe-tapping showtunes about Human Instrumentality :P (A good or a bad thing, depending on your point of view)

[]: Re: Musical Version Of NGE             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 28, 2016 6:11 pm For the longest time I've felt that "I. Shinji" would be hilarious and awesome if done opera-style.

[]: Re: Musical Version Of NGE             Quote Post by Director Black » Fri Oct 28, 2016 7:18 pm How about Lin Manuel Miranda to tell Eva like it was a history lesson like he did with Hamilton? More Eva raps can be needed (Not just for shitty AMVs).

[]: Re: Musical Version Of NGE             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Oct 28, 2016 9:01 pm Director Black wrote: How about Lin Manuel Miranda to tell Eva like it was a history lesson like he did with Hamilton? More Eva raps can be needed (Not just for shitty AMVs).

Article: Musical Version Of NGE
iving this thread from the dead (Evageeks seems to be a haven for ancient threads) How about a musical that focuses more on story, character and special effects than musical numbers? Any of the songs would be in the form of internal monologues exploring the characters' struggles and psychological states, thus making the musical of the "All In Their Heads" variety. Hell, maybe they can even be sung by non-actor singers who come on stage while the character actors continue going through the events and action (though having the characters sing could work too). Something like these: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=p0j5XWnb8aE https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5I9XkHgveyg So don't worry, Gendo won't be singing catchy, toe-tapping showtunes about Human Instrumentality :P (A good or a bad thing, depending on your point of view)
